channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Trump calls SCOTUS tariffs decision 'deeply disappointing' and lays out path forward
item.description: President Trump claimed the justices opposing his position were acting because of partisanship, though three of those ruling against his tariffs were ...

item.title: On pins and needles: Why Olympians pass the time knitting
item.description: A number of Olympic athletes have turned to knitting during the heat of the Games, including Ben Ogden, who this week became the most decorated Americ...

item.title: NASA eyes March 6 to launch 4 astronauts to the moon on Artemis II mission
item.description: The four astronauts heading to the moon for the lunar fly-by are the first humans to venture there since 1972. The ten-day mission will travel more th...

item.title: The U.S. men's hockey team to face Slovakia for a spot in an Olympic gold medal match
item.description: After an overtime nailbiter in the quarterfinals, the Americans return to the ice Friday in Milan to face the upstart Slovakia for a chance to play Ca...

item.title: U.K. considers cutting ex-Prince Andrew from line of succession over his Epstein ties
item.description: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, the British former prince, is being investigated on suspicion of misconduct in having shared confidential trade informatio...

item.title: US build-up of warships and fighter jets tracked near Iran
item.description: A second US aircraft carrier appears to be heading towards the Middle East as Washington increases its pressure on Iran.

item.title: NASA targets March for first moon mission by Artemis astronauts after fueling test success
How advanced is Iran's nuclear program? Here's what we know.
item.description: President Trump is pressuring Iran to either curtail its nuclear program or face military strikes, after Iran amassed a large stockpile of highly enri...

item.title: Tariffs ruling is major blow to Trump's second-term agenda
item.description: The Supreme Court has weakened Trump's hand in dealing with other nations, writes Anthony Zurcher.

item.title: Nasa targets early March to send humans back around the Moon
item.description: Nasa sets the launch date following a successful "wet dress rehearsal" of the Artemis II mission.

item.title: Supreme Court ruling against Trump tariffs will offer relief, businesses say
item.description: Businesses could be owed nearly $150 billion in refunds after the Supreme Court struck down tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic...

item.title: Supreme Court strikes down Trumpâs sweeping tariffs, upending central plank of his economic agenda
item.description: President Donald Trump says heâs imposing a temporary global tariff of 10% after his far-reaching tariff regimen was struck down by the Supreme Court.

item.title: Judge holds DOJ lawyer in contempt as tensions flare over immigration cases
item.description: A Minnesota federal judge ordered a government attorney to be held in civil contempt of court, further escalating tension between the judiciary and Tr...

item.title: Trump attacks Supreme Court over tariff ruling, announces new 10% global tariff
item.description: President Trump lashed out at the Supreme Court over its tariff decision, saying he was "absolutely ashamed" of the justices who ruled against him.

item.title: Dog trained to illegally dump rubbish, Italian authorities say
item.description: "Ingenuity can never become an alibi for incivility", the City of Catania warns.

item.title: Trump admin. unveils plans to restrict work permits for asylum-seekers
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday formally proposed a regulation that would dramatically restrict work permits for asylum-seekers.

item.title: Trump tariffs ripped up global trade order. What now?
item.description: If you think the Supreme Court ruling heralds a return to pre-Trump business as usual - think again.

item.title: All truckers and bus drivers will be required to take commercial driverâs license tests in English
